topics.title: Day 1: Penetration Testing Methodology
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 1: Penetration Testing Methodology is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Understand standard pentest methodologies
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Create a pentest engagement plan following PTES methodology
topics.interviewQuestion: What are the standard pentest methodologies?
topics.interviewAnswer: PTES (7 phases: Pre-engagement, Intelligence Gathering, Threat Modeling, Vulnerability Analysis, Exploitation, Post-Exploitation, Reporting), OWASP Testing Guide (web apps), OSSTMM (network), NIST SP 800-115. Pentest types: black box, white box, grey box.

quiz.question: How many phases in PTES?
What is black box testing?
What does OWASP Testing Guide cover?
What is step 1 of PTES?
What did the Jenkins server example show?
options: Anonymous access led to full compromise

quiz.question: Which best describes Day 1: Penetration Testing Methodology?
quiz.explanation: Day 1: Penetration Testing Methodology is a foundational cybersecurity concept that defines how systems work. It is a security principle, not a programming language or protocol.

quiz.question: What is the primary purpose of Day 1: Penetration Testing Methodology?
quiz.explanation: Security principles like those in Day 1: Penetration Testing Methodology typically span confidentiality, integrity, and availability.

quiz.question: Where does Day 1: Penetration Testing Methodology apply in real-world scenarios?
quiz.explanation: Day 1: Penetration Testing Methodology concepts are universal across all security domains.

quiz.question: Which certification exam covers Day 1: Penetration Testing Methodology?
quiz.explanation: Most major certifications touch on Day 1: Penetration Testing Methodology at some level.

quiz.question: Best way to gain hands-on experience with Day 1: Penetration Testing Methodology?
quiz.explanation: Practical application in a home lab is the most effective way to build skills. Reading alone is not enough.

quiz.question: Which skill is NOT directly related to Day 1: Penetration Testing Methodology?
quiz.explanation: While web development may use security concepts, Day 1: Penetration Testing Methodology is more directly tied to risk, response, and monitoring.

quiz.question: Scenario: You encounter Day 1: Penetration Testing Methodology during a security audit. What is your first step?
quiz.explanation: A professional approach to Day 1: Penetration Testing Methodology during an audit is to identify the issue and document findings for further analysis.

quiz.question: What is a common tool used with Day 1: Penetration Testing Methodology?
quiz.explanation: Depending on context, Day 1: Penetration Testing Methodology can involve firewalls, SIEMs, or packet analyzers.

quiz.question: Understanding Day 1: Penetration Testing Methodology helps prevent which type of attack?
quiz.explanation: The specific attack Day 1: Penetration Testing Methodology prevents depends on its context within the security domain.

quiz.question: How does Day 1: Penetration Testing Methodology relate to the CIA triad?
quiz.explanation: Most security concepts including Day 1: Penetration Testing Methodology can impact all three pillars of the CIA triad.

topics.title: Day 2: OWASP Testing Guide
topics.description: Apply OWASP testing methodology to web apps
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 2: OWASP Testing Guide is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Apply OWASP testing methodology to web apps
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Execute 3 OWASP test cases against DVWA or WebGoat
topics.interviewQuestion: What are the OWASP Testing Guide categories?
topics.interviewAnswer: 12 categories: Information Gathering, Configuration Management, Identity Management, Authentication, Authorization, Session Management, Input Validation, Error Handling, Cryptography, Business Logic, Client-side Testing, and API Testing. Each has specific test cases mapped to OWASP Top 10.

quiz.question: How many OWASP test categories?
WSTG-INPV-05 tests?
WSTG-INPV-01 tests?
What did the e-commerce audit find?
Where should tests be executed?

topics.title: Day 3: Web Application Reconnaissance
topics.description: Perform web app recon using multiple techniques
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 3: Web Application Reconnaissance is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Perform web app recon using multiple techniques
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Perform web reconnaissance on a practice target using dorking, directory brute force, and crawling
topics.interviewQuestion: What are web app reconnaissance techniques?
topics.interviewAnswer: Passive: Google dorking, Shodan, WHOIS, Wayback Machine. Active: directory brute force (gobuster), parameter discovery (Arjun), technology fingerprinting (Wappalyzer), subdomain enumeration (subfinder). Manual: explore application, map all pages and API endpoints.

quiz.question: What is passive recon?
options: Search engine dorking and public data

quiz.question: What is active recon?
options: Directory brute force and technology ID

quiz.question: What does gobuster do?
What did the pentester find via Google dork?
What is Wappalyzer?

topics.title: Day 4: Vulnerability Analysis & Exploitation
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 4: Vulnerability Analysis & Exploitation is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Analyze
- Exploit web vulnerabilities
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Identify technology versions, find CVEs, and exploit vulnerabilities in a vulnerable web app
topics.interviewQuestion: What is the vulnerability analysis process?
topics.interviewAnswer: Identify technology versions, search for known CVEs (searchsploit, NVD), verify the vulnerability is present, assess exploitability (PoC available, auth required), and prioritize based on CVSS and asset criticality. Common exploits: SQLi, command injection, LFI/RFI, IDOR, SSRF.

quiz.question: What tool searches for known CVEs?
What is required before exploiting?
What vulnerability did Apache 2.4.49 have?
What is LFI?
What did the pentester chain with path traversal?

topics.title: Day 5: Pentest Reporting & Remediation Project
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 5: Pentest Reporting & Remediation Project is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Write professional penetration test reports
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Write a complete pentest report with executive summary, findings, and remediation roadmap
topics.interviewQuestion: What does a professional pentest report include?
topics.interviewAnswer: Executive summary (1-2 pages for leadership), technical findings organized by severity with CVSS scores and PoC, methodology, scope, risk scoring, and remediation roadmap. Tools: Serpico, Ghostwriter. Findings include: description, affected asset, CVSS, PoC, remediation, and references.

quiz.question: Who is the executive summary for?
What tools help generate reports?
What goes in technical findings?
How many findings did the fintech report have?
How fast were Critical issues fixed?
